tests.description: Cold power-on starts the wake-up counter at zero
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Wakeup count: 0' on the first boot; the counter is not incremented for the initial power-on.
tests.notes: Requires: serial console. Cold boot = fully remove power (battery + USB) then re-apply, not the reset button. xWakeUpCounter is RTC-persisted and only clears on full power loss.

tests.description: Each measurement-cycle deep-sleep wake-up increments the wake-up counter
tests.expected_result: After a measurement-cycle timer wake-up, serial logs 'Wakeup count: N' with N incremented by 1 versus the previous measurement cycle.
tests.notes: Counter lives in RTC memory (RTC_DATA_ATTR), so it survives deep sleep but resets on power loss.

tests.description: Serial console is re-initialized after a deep-sleep wake-up
tests.expected_result: Logging resumes after every deep-sleep timer wake-up. On measurement-cycle wake-ups, 'Wakeup count: N' appears after roughly a 1s post-wake delay. Fragmented-sleep wake-ups log sleep scheduling and return to sleep without 'Wakeup count'.
tests.notes: Console re-init runs only when the wake cause is not UNDEFINED; on cold boot the console is already up so this path is skipped.

tests.description: Device enters deep sleep after the measurement and transmission cycle completes
tests.expected_result: At end of cycle serial logs 'Next measurements is in <N> seconds' then 'Sleeping for for <N> seconds' (literal double 'for'), the LED turns off, then the device deep-sleeps.
tests.notes: Per-chunk sleep is capped at 300s; a longer schedule re-wakes for fragmented sleep (see boot.fragmented-sleep.*).

tests.description: External hardware watchdog is kicked at boot and before each sleep
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Watchdog reset' at boot and again before every deep sleep.
tests.notes: Requires: serial console. The external WDT forces a reset if not kicked within its window; fragmented sleep exists specifically to kick it during long idle periods.

tests.description: A sleep longer than 5 minutes is split into chunks no longer than 300s
tests.expected_result: When the next measurement is more than 300s away, serial logs 'Next measurement is scheduled in more than 5 minutes. Will do fragmented sleep to reset the external watchdog timer before returning to sleep again', then sleeps 300s and wakes only to kick the watchdog.
tests.notes: Easiest to trigger on low battery, where the measure interval becomes 1800s or 3600s (see Power Management). MAX_SLEEP_TIME (300s) is the external-watchdog ceiling.

tests.description: Fragmented-sleep wake-ups do not increment the wake-up counter
tests.expected_result: During fragmented sleep serial logs 'Next measurements is in <N> seconds' and 'Sleeping for <N> seconds' then re-sleeps, with no 'Wakeup count' line for that intermediate wake-up.
tests.notes: Guarded by xMeasurementLeadTimeSeconds>0; the device re-enters deep sleep before reaching the counter-increment step, so only real measurement wake-ups are counted.

tests.description: LED turns solid ON shortly after cold power-on
tests.expected_result: The LED indicator turns solid ON shortly after firmware starts and status LED initialization completes, and stays on until the next notification in the boot sequence.
tests.notes: Cold boot only (gated by wake-up counter == 0); the LED is not turned solid-on for deep-sleep wake-ups.

tests.description: Quick blink 2x when network initialization starts
tests.expected_result: 2 quick blinks (~100ms interval over 400ms) when the networking task begins network initialization. Serial logs 'Time for transmission, run networking tasks...' before the task is started.
tests.notes: First boot only. Applies to both cellular (CE card) and WiFi init paths.

tests.description: Quick blink 3x on successful network connection
tests.expected_result: 3 quick blinks (~100ms interval over 600ms) on successful network connection; on the ensure-connection path serial logs 'Client connection is ready'.
tests.notes: Cellular mode (the default network option); the WiFi path does not emit this 3x indicator.

tests.description: Quick blink 4x after first successful measurement post
tests.expected_result: 4 quick blinks (~100ms interval over 800ms) after the first measurement is posted to the server successfully.
tests.notes: First boot only. Fires on both cellular (CoAP) and WiFi (HTTP) post-success paths.

tests.description: LED stays off during a normal wake-up cycle
tests.expected_result: On a normal deep-sleep wake-up the LED stays off for the entire cycle (no solid-on, no blink) unless an error indication fires.
tests.notes: Contrast with cold boot, where the LED is solid-on; the success-sequence blinks are first-boot only.

tests.description: Blink 5x when toggling the System Settings Portal via boot button
tests.expected_result: 5 blinks (~200ms interval over 2000ms) then the device restarts; serial logs 'Restarting in 2s...'.
tests.notes: Triggered by a 3s+ boot-button hold (see Boot Button category); toggles the portal on or off.

tests.description: Continuous quick blink while the portal is active
tests.expected_result: The LED blinks continuously (~100ms interval, no end) the whole time the System Settings Portal is running.
tests.notes: Indefinite blink (duration 0); stops when the portal exits, saves, or is aborted.

tests.description: Slow blink 2x when one or more sensors fail to initialize
tests.expected_result: 2 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 2000ms); serial logs 'One or more sensor were failed to initialize, will not measure those on this iteration'.
tests.notes: Fires on first boot and on every wake-up (not cycle-gated). Reproduce by disconnecting a sensor before boot.

tests.description: Slow blink 3x when a measurement post fails
tests.expected_result: 3 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 3000ms) when posting measures fails.
tests.notes: Requires: network, WiFi, or cellular post failure. On wake-up cycles serial logs 'Send measures failed, retry in next schedule'; on first boot when the client is not ready it logs 'Send measures failed because of client is not ready, ensuring connection...'.

tests.description: Slow blink 4x when a post fails due to a server issue
tests.expected_result: 4 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 4000ms); serial logs 'Send measures failed because of server issue, retry in next schedule'.
tests.notes: Cellular first boot only. Requires server-side rejection or registration/server issue path; hard to reproduce.

tests.description: Slow blink 5x when ensuring connection fails after a failed post
tests.expected_result: 5 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 5000ms) then the device restarts; serial logs 'Failed ensuring client connection, system restart in 5s'.
tests.notes: Requires: network failure on first boot (hard to reproduce); cellular path only.

tests.description: Slow blink 6x after giving up post retries on first boot
tests.expected_result: 6 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 6000ms) then the device restarts; serial logs 'Give up after 3 attempts of failed post measures because of network reasons, restart systems in 6s'.
tests.notes: Requires: persistent network failure on first boot (hard to reproduce); fires after 3 failed post attempts with the client not ready.

tests.description: Slow blink 10x while retrying network connection on first boot
tests.expected_result: 10 slow blinks (~500ms interval over 10000ms) between connection retries; serial logs 'Airgradient client still not connected, retry in 10s'.
tests.notes: Requires: no cellular coverage on first boot (hard to reproduce); part of the ensure-connection loop that runs up to 1 hour.

tests.description: Holding the boot button longer than 3s then releasing toggles the System Settings Portal
tests.expected_result: On release after a >3s hold, serial logs 'Restarting in 2s...', the LED blinks 5x (~200ms over 2s), then the device restarts and boots into the System Settings Portal.
tests.notes: First boot / normal-run only. The trigger fires on button RELEASE after holding >3s, not while held. The portal is off by default, so the first such hold enables it. NOTE: README and the old template said 5s, but firmware uses >3000ms.

tests.description: Holding the boot button longer than 3s again while the portal is active disables the portal setting and restarts
tests.expected_result: A second >3s hold-and-release while the portal is running logs 'Restarting in 2s...', toggles `runSystemSettings` off, restarts, and the device boots normally with the portal disabled.
tests.notes: Applies when the portal was enabled by the previous boot-button hold. This is a boot-button toggle/restart path, not the WiFiManager Exit button abort path.

tests.description: Boot button has no effect after a deep-sleep wake-up
tests.expected_result: Pressing or holding the boot button on any wake-up cycle produces no portal toggle, no restart, and no LED change.
tests.notes: The button ISR and handler task are installed only on first boot (wake-up counter == 0); on later wake-ups they are never registered.

tests.description: Portal AP broadcasts the expected SSID and password
tests.expected_result: The AP SSID is 'airgradient-<serial>' (hyphen, lowercase 12-hex serial) with password 'cleanair'; serial logs 'Connect to WiFi network: airgradient-<serial>'.
tests.notes: Serial = lowercase WiFi-STA MAC (12 hex chars). The old template said 'airgradient_<SERIAL>' with an underscore, which is incorrect.

tests.description: Captive-portal detection redirects clients to the portal
tests.expected_result: Known captive-portal probe routes respond as implemented: /generate_204 returns 204, /ncsi.txt returns 'Microsoft NCSI', and /hotspot-detect.html plus /fwlink return 302 redirect to '/'.
tests.notes: Captive pop-up behavior depends on OS/device. On Android the pop-up may not appear; open http://192.168.4.1 manually.

tests.description: Portal home page reachable at 192.168.4.1
tests.expected_result: Browsing to http://192.168.4.1 serves the 'Device Portal' home page; serial logs 'Open browser to: http://192.168.4.1'.
tests.notes: 192.168.4.1 is the AP gateway IP; works regardless of whether the captive pop-up triggered.

tests.description: Portal times out after 180s, restarts, and re-runs the portal
tests.expected_result: With no save or exit, after 180s serial logs 'Config portal timeout', the device restarts, and the portal runs again.
tests.notes: Timeout leaves runSystemSettings unchanged, so a boot-button-enabled portal re-runs after restart. If the portal is running because WiFi mode is unconfigured, it can also re-run due to isWifiConfigured=false.

tests.description: Exit button closes the portal and the device runs normally
tests.expected_result: Pressing Exit serves 'Exiting WiFi Manager', the device restarts and boots into the normal flow with the portal disabled.
tests.notes: Applies when the portal was enabled by runSystemSettings/boot button. If the portal is running because WiFi mode is selected but not configured, the portal may re-run until WiFi is configured.

tests.description: Info button shows device information
tests.expected_result: The 'Info' link opens /info and displays the device/ESP32 information page.
tests.notes: Served from handleInfo.

tests.description: System Settings button opens the settings form
tests.expected_result: The 'System Settings' link opens /settings (the configuration form page).
tests.notes: Served from handleSettings.

tests.description: Home page shows live WiFi connection status
tests.expected_result: The home page auto-fetches /status and shows 'Connected to: <ssid> / IP Address: <ip>' when connected, or 'Not connected to: <saved_ssid>' / 'No WiFi configured' otherwise.
tests.notes: Most meaningful in WiFi mode after a save attempt.

tests.description: Settings form is prefilled with the saved configuration
tests.expected_result: Opening the settings page prefills net_mode, HTTP URL Domain, CoAP Host, SSID, and APN from the current config (fetched from /fetch-settings).
tests.notes: max.cpp seeds these via setSettings() before the portal starts (networkMode, apn, httpDomain, coapDomain).

tests.description: WiFi section lists available networks
tests.expected_result: In WiFi mode the 'Available Networks' list populates from a scan (/scan); networks below 8% signal quality are filtered out.
tests.notes: CONFIG_WM_MIN_SIGNAL_QUALITY = 8; duplicate SSIDs may be removed depending on CONFIG_WM_REMOVE_DUP_APS.

tests.description: Refresh link re-scans for networks
tests.expected_result: Clicking 'Refresh' triggers a new /scan and updates the Available Networks list.
tests.notes: Calls loadNetworks() in settings.html.

tests.description: Network-mode toggle shows the matching settings section
tests.expected_result: Selecting WiFi shows the WiFi section (SSID/password); selecting Cellular shows the Cellular section (APN + CoAP Host).
tests.notes: toggleNetworkSettings() hides/shows the two form sections.

tests.description: Selecting a scanned network fills SSID and focuses password
tests.expected_result: Clicking a network in the list fills the SSID field with its name and moves keyboard focus to the password field.
tests.notes: selectNetwork() sets #s value and calls #p.focus().

tests.description: Saving in WiFi mode starts a connection attempt
tests.expected_result: On Save with mode=WiFi the page shows 'Connecting...' and auto-returns to home after 5s; serial logs 'Attempting to connect to WiFi...'.
tests.notes: Empty SSID is rejected with 'Error: SSID required'.

tests.description: Successful WiFi connection sets WiFi mode and continues without restart
tests.expected_result: On success serial logs 'STA got IP: <ip>'; the portal closes, network mode is set to WiFi (isWifiConfigured=true), and the device continues the normal flow WITHOUT restarting.
tests.notes: Contrast with cellular save, which restarts. WiFi success continues in-place (g_networkReady set true).

tests.description: Failed WiFi connection times out, restarts, and re-runs the portal
tests.expected_result: If the connection fails within ~30s, serial logs 'STA connection timeout', startConfigPortal returns failure, the device restarts, and the portal runs again because WiFi is still not configured or runSystemSettings remains enabled.
tests.notes: Connect timeout CONFIG_WM_DEFAULT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT = 30s; state goes to START_PORTAL, the blocking portal loop exits with failure, and max.cpp restarts the device.

tests.description: Saving in cellular mode shows the wait-and-check message
tests.expected_result: On Save with mode=Cellular the page shows 'Configuration Saved!' with 'Please wait and check if device able to communicate with our server'.
tests.notes: Non-empty APN shows the success/wait message. Empty APN sends 'Error: APN required for cellular network', but this path should be treated as an implementation edge case.

tests.description: Cellular save persists APN, sets cellular mode, and restarts into normal flow
tests.expected_result: After cellular save the network mode is set to Cellular, the new APN is persisted, and the device restarts into the normal flow; the saved APN is then used for cellular registration.
tests.notes: Cellular path restarts (max.cpp esp_restart), unlike WiFi which continues in-place. Verify APN use via serial AT logs / successful registration.

tests.description: Cellular section exposes a CoAP Host field whose value is saved
tests.expected_result: The Cellular section shows a 'CoAP Host (IP or Domain)' field (placeholder 128.140.49.53); the entered value persists after Save and is used as the CoAP target on the next boot.
tests.notes: NEW since the old template: the CoAP-domain field was recently moved into the cellular settings section. Transmission effect is covered in the Transmission - Configuration category.

tests.description: Serial number is printed at boot
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Serial number: <serial>' where <serial> is the 12-hex-char lowercase WiFi-STA MAC.
tests.notes: Used as the device identifier for transmission and the portal AP SSID.

tests.description: Firmware version is printed at boot
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Firmware version: <version>' matching the flashed build's app description version.
tests.notes: Sourced from the app description; cross-check against the released version under test.

tests.description: ESP reset reason is printed at boot
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Reset reason: ESP_RST_POWERON' on cold boot and 'Reset reason: ESP_RST_SW' after a software restart (e.g. a portal save or post-failure restart).
tests.notes: Handled reasons print their enum names, including ESP_RST_POWERON, ESP_RST_SW, ESP_RST_WDT, and ESP_RST_BROWNOUT. Any unhandled reset reason prints 'Reset reason: unknown'.

tests.description: ESP wake-up reason is printed at boot
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Wakeup caused by timer' on a timer wake-up; on cold boot it logs 'Wakeup was not caused by deep sleep: <n>'.
tests.notes: Deep-sleep wake on the MAX is timer-based; EXT0/EXT1/touch/ULP causes are not used.

tests.description: Boot diagnostics are re-printed on every wake-up cycle
tests.expected_result: Each measurement-cycle wake-up re-prints 'Wakeup count: N', the reset and wake-up reason lines, and the '**** CONFIGURATION ****' block.
tests.notes: Confirms the console is re-initialized each cycle; the config block comes from Configuration::load() which runs every boot.

tests.description: A sensor that fails to initialize or is not connected is excluded from the measurement sequence
tests.expected_result: On init failure the matching log prints (e.g. 'Failed open serial for Sunlight', 'Failed init temperature and humidity sensor', 'Failed init TVOC & NOx sensor', 'Failed init charger', 'PMS1 is not connected') and that sensor's values remain at invalid defaults instead of being included in averaging.
tests.notes: Each sensor read is guarded by its availability flag; a failed sensor keeps its iteration OK-count at 0 so it is skipped in averaging. Reproduce by disconnecting one sensor.

tests.description: O3/NO2 sensor is only initialized on the O3/NO2-capable model
tests.expected_result: On the O-M-1PPSTON-CE model the AlphaSense O3/NO2 sensor is initialized and contributes O3/NO2 values when available; on the base model serial logs 'Skip O3/NO2 sensor, not supported on this model' and O3/NO2/AFE values remain invalid defaults.
tests.notes: Requires checking both models: O-M-1PPSTON-CE (with O3/NO2) and O-M-1PPST-CE (without). Model comes from remote config; default is O-M-1PPSTON-CE.

tests.description: CO2 sensor single-mode configuration runs once and is skipped on later boots
tests.expected_result: On first configuration serial logs 'Setting measurement mode to single mode...'; on subsequent boots it logs 'Configuration already completed, skipping setup...'.
tests.notes: Tracked by an RTC-memory flag (rtc_samples_configured), so it persists across deep sleep after successful first configuration. If the first configuration does not save the flag, setup may run again.

tests.description: PM and SGP sensors run a warm-up sequence before measurements
tests.expected_result: Before the measurement loop serial logs a countdown 'Warming up PMS and/or SGP41 sensors 10' down to '...0' (about 11s).
tests.notes: The countdown log runs every init. PMS passive-mode calls and SGP41 conditioning only run for sensors marked available; SGP41 also runs a self-test first.

tests.description: Measurement sequence runs 20 iterations before averaging
tests.expected_result: Serial logs 'Start measures with 20 iterations and interval in between 2000ms' followed by 20 'Iteration <n> took ...' lines, then the average is computed.
tests.notes: DEFAULT_MEASURE_ITERATION_COUNT=20, 2000ms target interval per iteration; the average uses only iterations with valid data per sensor.

tests.description: CO2 is sampled 9 times across the 20-iteration sequence
tests.expected_result: Across the 20 iterations the CO2 sensor is read 9 times (debug 'CO2: <n>' read lines at odd iterations 3,5,7,9,11,13,15,17,19).
tests.notes: CO2 runs in single mode: a trigger on one odd iteration is read on the next odd iteration (3s settle), so the first odd iteration only triggers. Requires DEBUG-level serial output (init sets the Sensor tag to DEBUG).

tests.description: Two PM sensors are averaged, with single-sensor fallback
tests.expected_result: When both PMS read successfully, PM1.0/PM10 and shared particle counts except PM0.3 are averaged across the two; PM2.5 and PM0.3 particle count are kept per channel. If only one PMS reads, its values are used; if a PMS fails it logs '{1} PMS no data' or '{2} PMS no data'.
tests.notes: Reproduce single-sensor mode by disconnecting one PMS.

tests.description: Averaged measurements are logged after the sequence
tests.expected_result: After averaging, serial logs an '<<< Average Measures >>>' block listing CO2, Temperature, Humidity, PM, particle counts, TVOC/NOx Raw, VBAT, VPanel, O3/NO2 electrodes, and AFE temperature.
tests.notes: O3/NO2 and AFE lines are always printed; on the base model or when AlphaSense is unavailable they remain invalid defaults. The average block logs raw SGP41 values only; calculated TVOC/NOx indexes are logged after averaging.

tests.description: SGP41 raw VOC/NOx values are converted to calculated gas-index values
tests.expected_result: After measurement, serial logs 'TVOC Raw : <n>' and 'NOx Raw : <n>' in the average block, then logs 'TVOC index: <n>' and 'NOx index: <n>'. With valid SGP41 readings, transmission includes both raw and calculated index values.
tests.notes: Requires SGP41 available. If SHT temperature/humidity is invalid, firmware uses default 25°C / 50%RH compensation so SGP41 raw reads can continue. The gas-index algorithm state is stored in RTC memory so it continues learning across deep sleep.

tests.description: Cellular CE card is powered only on transmission cycles
tests.expected_result: On a non-transmission cellular wake-up the CE card stays powered off and no networking task starts; serial logs 'Not the time to send measures, skip'. On transmission cycles the CE card is powered for the transmission then powered off afterward.
tests.notes: Send-measures runs every 3rd wake-up (SEND_MEASURES_CYCLES=3) and full transmission every 30th (FULL_TRANSMISSION_CYCLE=30). WiFi mode connects every cycle instead.

tests.description: CO2 and AlphaSense load switches stay powered across deep sleep
tests.expected_result: EN_CO2 and EN_ALPHASENSE are driven ON at first boot and latched via gpio_hold so they remain powered through deep sleep and every subsequent wake-up.
tests.notes: Re-driven only on first boot; on later wake-ups they are excluded from GPIO reconfiguration to keep the held state. Old template observed ~5.1V on this rail during sleep.

tests.description: PM sensors are powered off after measurement before sleeping
tests.expected_result: After the measurement sequence the PM load switches (EN_PMS1/EN_PMS2) are turned off before the device enters deep sleep.
tests.notes: PM sensors are powered only during warm-up and measurement; the CE card is enabled in parallel by the networking task rather than after PM shutdown.

tests.description: Measurement interval lengthens as battery voltage drops
tests.expected_result: The measure interval follows battery voltage: at 10V or above it uses the configured pm02 interval (default 180s), 9.5V to under 10V uses 1800s (30 min), and under 9.5V uses 3600s (60 min); the chosen interval drives the next-measurement schedule.
tests.notes: Requires varying battery voltage (hard to reproduce). First boot logs 'Measure interval is set to <N> as base line'. If battery voltage is invalid (<0), the configured pm02 interval is used.

tests.description: Sleep duration is reduced by the time spent awake to keep a consistent cadence
tests.expected_result: The next-measurement schedule equals the measure interval minus the time spent awake this cycle, clamped to 0 seconds; serial logs 'Next measurements is in <N> seconds' reflecting that compensation.
tests.notes: Each sleep chunk is capped at 300s; longer waits use fragmented sleep (see Boot & Sleep Lifecycle).

tests.description: Panel/VBUS voltage prefers the solar input over USB-C
tests.expected_result: The panel voltage (VPanel in the average-measures log) is read from ADC1 (solar) when its reading is >= 1000 mV, otherwise it falls back to ADC2 (USB-C).
tests.notes: Requires switching between solar and USB-C power (hard to reproduce). Reflected in the 'VPanel' value of the '<<< Average Measures >>>' log.

tests.description: Remote configuration is fetched on first boot and every full-transmission cycle
tests.expected_result: Remote config is fetched on first boot and then every 30th wake-up (~90 min at the default 180s interval) when the network/client is ready. On other cycles serial logs 'Not the time to fetch remote configuration, skip'.
tests.notes: FULL_TRANSMISSION_CYCLE=30; wall-clock spacing grows when the measure interval increases on low battery. CORRECTION: the old template said 'every 1 hour' - actual baseline is ~90 min.

tests.description: Fetched persisted configuration changes take effect on the next wake-up
tests.expected_result: When a persisted config change is fetched, serial logs 'Changed configuration will be applied on next wakeup cycle onwards'; those persisted values are used from the next wake-up, not the current measurement/transmission flow.
tests.notes: Excludes volatile one-shot fields such as ledBarTestRequested, which is not persisted to NVS. Changes are saved to NVS during fetch; the running cycle keeps the config it loaded at its own boot.

tests.description: Configuration persists across a full power cycle
tests.expected_result: After power-off then power-on, the previously fetched/saved settings are still active, visible in the '**** CONFIGURATION ****' block.
tests.notes: Stored in NVS (flash), unlike RTC memory which clears on power loss.

tests.description: co2CalibrationRequested triggers a one-time CO2 manual calibration
tests.expected_result: When co2CalibrationRequested is set, the device runs CO2 manual calibration on the next boot (serial logs 'Attempt to do manual calibration') then clears the request so it does not repeat.
tests.notes: Set from the dashboard; the request is reset after the attempt regardless of success.

tests.description: ledBarTestRequested keeps the status LED solid ON
tests.expected_result: When ledBarTestRequested is set, after the measurement cycle the LED is turned solid ON and latched (gpio_hold) so it stays lit until the device is power-cycled; serial logs 'Led test is requested' when the field is parsed.
tests.notes: The JSON field is 'ledBarTestRequested'. This request is not persisted to NVS.

tests.description: mqttBrokerUrl enables or disables MQTT publishing
tests.expected_result: When mqttBrokerUrl is set, measures are also published via MQTT after a successful cellular post. When the field is absent during config fetch, any saved MQTT URL is cleared; on subsequent successful cellular send cycles with an empty MQTT URL, serial logs 'MQTT is not enabled, skip'.
tests.notes: Removing the field logs 'Previously mqtt is enabled. Disabling it because now its not found'. MQTT is attempted only on the cellular send path, after HTTP/CoAP success.

tests.description: Configured HTTP and CoAP domains are used as transmission targets
tests.expected_result: HTTP requests go to the configured httpDomain and cellular CoAP measures go to the configured coapDomain instead of the defaults.
tests.notes: Both are set via the System Settings Portal (not remote config) and persisted in NVS; defaults are AIRGRADIENT_HTTP_DOMAIN / AIRGRADIENT_COAP_IP. Verify via serial logs or server receipt.

tests.description: cellularWarmUpMs controls modem warm-up after registration
tests.expected_result: On the normal cellular registration path, the modem warm-up duration after successful network registration follows the configured cellularWarmUpMs value.
tests.notes: Cellular only. Parsing logs 'cellularWarmUpMs value changed from <a> to <b>'; precise duration is hard to observe directly. Reconnection/ensure-client paths may also use fixed warm-up delays.

tests.description: abcDays controls CO2 automatic background calibration
tests.expected_result: abcDays > 0 enables ABC with a period of abcDays*24 hours; abcDays <= 0 disables ABC. Serial logs 'CO2 ABC status: <0|1>' at sensor init.
tests.notes: Combines the old separate ABC-value and ABC-toggle tests. Parsing logs 'abcDays value changed from <a> to <b>'.

tests.description: Firmware update is checked on first boot and every full-transmission cycle
tests.expected_result: An OTA check runs on first boot and then every 30th wake-up (~90 min at the default 180s interval) when the network/client is ready. On other cycles serial logs 'Not the time to check for firmware update, skip'.
tests.notes: Same FULL_TRANSMISSION_CYCLE=30 gating as config fetch. CORRECTION: the old template said 'every 1 hour' - actual baseline is ~90 min.

tests.description: No update is applied when firmware is already the latest
tests.expected_result: When the running firmware is current the server check returns HTTP 304; serial logs 'Firmware is already up to date' then 'Firmware version already up to date', and the device continues without updating.
tests.notes: Reproduce by running the version the dashboard is targeting for this device.

tests.description: Update is skipped when the server declines the request
tests.expected_result: When the server returns a non-200/304 status (e.g. 400), serial logs 'Firmware update skipped, the server returned <code>' then 'Firmware update is skipped', and no update is applied.
tests.notes: Distinct from a transport failure; the request reached the server but was declined.

tests.description: A failed update is retried on a later cycle without restarting
tests.expected_result: When the download or connection fails, serial logs 'Firmware update failed', the device does NOT restart, and the check is retried on the next full-transmission cycle.
tests.notes: Reproduce by targeting an available firmware then interrupting connectivity during download (hard to reproduce reliably).

tests.description: A successful update restarts into the new firmware
tests.expected_result: On a successful update serial logs 'Firmware update success, will restart in 3s...', the device restarts after ~3s, and the post-boot 'Firmware version: <version>' log shows the new version.
tests.notes: Reproduce by targeting a different available firmware version on the dashboard (e.g. a downgrade).

tests.description: In WiFi mode the device connects on every wake-up
tests.expected_result: When network mode is WiFi, the networking task runs every wake-up cycle and reconnects to WiFi each time, regardless of the send/full-transmission cycle counters.
tests.notes: Requires: device configured to WiFi mode via the System Settings Portal (default is Cellular). Unlike cellular, WiFi is not gated by SEND_MEASURES_CYCLES.

tests.description: WiFi measures are sent directly after the measurement sequence
tests.expected_result: After each measurement sequence the device posts a single measurement set over HTTP; on successful posts, a new reading appears on the dashboard every wake-up (~3 min at the default 180s interval).
tests.notes: Requires: WiFi mode. Each post contains exactly one measurement set (no batching).

tests.description: WiFi mode does not cache or resend failed measurements
tests.expected_result: If WiFi is connected but the HTTP post fails, serial logs 'Send measures failed, retry in next schedule' and that measurement set is dropped; the next cycle sends fresh data only, never the previously failed payload.
tests.notes: Requires: WiFi mode. Caching is cellular-only; the 'retry' wording means a new measurement next cycle, not a resend of the failed one. If WiFi connection fails before posting, the skip log is 'Network is not ready, skip send measures'.

tests.description: WiFi is disconnected after the transmission cycle
tests.expected_result: Once the networking task finishes, serial logs 'Disconnecting from WiFi..' then 'Wi-Fi disconnected successfully' before the device sleeps.
tests.notes: Requires: WiFi mode. The station connection is disconnected before sleep; current implementation does not explicitly stop/deinit the WiFi driver.

tests.description: Operators are scanned when no cached operator list exists
tests.expected_result: When the saved operator list is empty, serial logs 'No operator list available, continue to: SCAN_OPERATOR' then 'Scanning for available operators'; with a cached list it logs 'Operator list available (<n> operators)' and skips scanning.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode. The list is empty on first boot or after a full registration failure clears it.

tests.description: Registration walks the operator list until one succeeds
tests.expected_result: The module tries operators in order, logging 'Configuring manual operator: <id> with AcT: <n> (index <i> of <m>)' for each, and stops at the first that registers.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode with multiple reachable operators to observe more than one attempt.

tests.description: Registration gives up after 3 full passes through the operator list
tests.expected_result: Each full exhaustion logs 'Operator list exhausted (attempt <n> of 3)' and resets the module between passes; after the 3rd pass it logs 'Failed after 3 full iterations through operator list', clears the list, and fails registration.
tests.notes: Requires: no operator able to register (hard to reproduce). MAX_REGISTRATION_FAILURES=3.

tests.description: Operators with CSQ below 10 are skipped
tests.expected_result: When a registered operator reports CSQ < 10, serial logs 'This operator <id> has really low signal <n> (csq), moving on..' and the module advances to the next operator.
tests.notes: Requires: a weak-signal operator (hard to reproduce). Valid CSQ range checked is 1-31; below 10 is rejected.

tests.description: Registration-denied/emergency-only operators fail fast after a 10s confirmation
tests.expected_result: On registration status 3 or 11, serial logs 'Registration denied or emergency only (status=<n>), confirming for 10 seconds'; if still denied after 10s it logs 'Registration still denied/emergency (status=<n>) after 10s, trying next operator'.
tests.notes: Requires: an operator that denies registration (hard to reproduce). If status changes to registered during the 10s window it proceeds instead.

tests.description: The last used operator is remembered and tried first on later wake-ups
tests.expected_result: On a subsequent wake-up the saved operator ID/list is loaded. Firmware either logs 'Found current operator at index <i>' while loading the cached list, or logs 'Searching for saved operator <id> in list' and 'Found saved operator at index <i>, trying it first'; the saved operator is attempted before starting from a fresh scan.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode across at least two wake-ups. The operator id/list is persisted to NVS via the configuration after each registration.

tests.description: Measures are transmitted every 3rd wake-up
tests.expected_result: Each measurement-cycle wake-up caches a measurement. Cellular transmission happens on first boot and then on wake-up counts that are multiples of SEND_MEASURES_CYCLES=3; on in-between cycles serial logs 'Not the time to send measures, skip (cache size: <n>)'.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode. At the default 180s interval this is roughly every 9 minutes, sending the 3 accumulated sets.

tests.description: Failed transmissions are cached and resent on the next cycle
tests.expected_result: When a CoAP post fails the cached measurements are retained (not cleared) and resent together with new readings on the next send cycle, so a payload may contain more than 3 sets after a missed transmission.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode with an induced transmission failure. The cache is volatile (RTC-backed) and is lost on full power loss.

tests.description: MQTT publish is attempted only after a successful CoAP post and only when enabled
tests.expected_result: After a successful CoAP post, if mqttBrokerUrl is set the device also publishes via MQTT; if it is not set serial logs 'MQTT is not enabled, skip' and the cache is cleared.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode. MQTT is never attempted when the CoAP post failed.

tests.description: Cellular first boot can retry post failures caused by client/network readiness
tests.expected_result: On first boot, if posting fails because the client is not ready, firmware attempts to ensure the client connection and can retry within the cycle, up to 3 post attempts. Server-side post failure breaks the loop. On later wake-ups, a failed post logs 'Send measures failed, retry in next schedule' and defers to the next send cycle.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode with an induced failure. First-boot retries also drive the slow-blink LED error codes (see LED Notification).

tests.description: An MQTT failure does not cause HTTP to resend already-sent measures
tests.expected_result: If MQTT publish fails, serial logs 'Retry publish on next schedule' and xHttpCacheQueueIndex is advanced to the current cache size, so already-sent measures are not re-posted over HTTP/CoAP on the next send cycle. The retained cache remains available for the next MQTT publish attempt.
tests.notes: Requires: cellular mode with MQTT enabled and an induced MQTT failure. Tracked by xHttpCacheQueueIndex.

tests.description: CO2 reading tracks a reference instrument
tests.expected_result: The averaged CO2 value (serial 'CO2 : <n>' and dashboard rco2) tracks a co-located reference CO2 instrument within the agreed tolerance across a few measurement cycles.
tests.notes: Requires: reference CO2 instrument; optional, skippable on rapid releases. Fresh-air baseline is ~400-420 ppm; allow warm-up. Manual calibration is available via co2CalibrationRequested.

tests.description: PM2.5 reading tracks a reference instrument
tests.expected_result: Averaged PM values (serial 'PM2.5#AE{1}/{2}' and dashboard pm25) track a co-located reference PM instrument within the agreed tolerance.
tests.notes: Requires: reference PM instrument and a controlled aerosol source; optional, skippable. Two PMS channels are averaged - both should read consistently.

tests.description: Temperature and humidity track a reference instrument
tests.expected_result: Averaged temperature ('Temperature : <n>', atmp) and humidity ('Humidity : <n>', rhum) track a co-located reference within the agreed tolerance.
tests.notes: Requires: reference temp/humidity instrument; optional, skippable. Allow thermal settling; self-heating can bias temperature.

tests.description: O3/NO2 AlphaSense electrode readings are plausible
tests.expected_result: On the O3/NO2 model the O3/NO2 working/auxiliary electrode voltages (serial 'O3 WE/AE', 'NO2 WE/AE' in mV) read within the sensor's expected baseline range and respond to gas exposure.
tests.notes: Requires: O-M-1PPSTON-CE model and gas reference setup (hard to source); optional, skippable. Base model has no O3/NO2 sensor.
